title: veronica orchidea subsp. crassifolia
intro: Veronica orchidea subsp. crassifolia, also known as the thick-leaved speedwell, is a plant species within the plantain family (Plantaginaceae) that is native to various parts of Europe and Asia. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, allowing it to be used to treat skin irritations and wounds. The health benefits of Veronica orchidea subsp. crassifolia include soothing and calming the skin, reducing inflammation, and promoting wound healing. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, and can also be used in salves and ointments to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
